+
+102. **Add an editor-shell extension-command executor seam.**
+   Implement the smallest execution-policy step beyond the existing extension
+   command capture FIFO. Current source shows `Command::Custom` and
+   `Command::Plugin` menu activations already reach
+   `EditorShell::route_menu_command`, which captures them into
+   `extension_menu_commands`; task 102 should add the shell-owned seam that can
+   execute those captured commands through an injected handler, without wiring
+   real plugin runtime/discovery/loading.
+
+   **Scope / MAY edit:**
+   - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
+   - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/extension_command.rs` (new, optional)
+   - `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`
+   - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`
+   - `plans/BASELINE.md`
+   - `Status.md`
+   - `HANDOFF.md`
+   - `change.md`
+   - `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+   - generated ISSUE-102 handoff/audit/log artifacts for this dispatch only
+
+   **MUST NOT edit:**
+   - `crates/editor-ui/**`
+   - `crates/editor-egui-host/**`
+   - `kernel/plugin-host/**`
+   - `crates/plugin-*`
+   - `plugins/**`
+   - `runtime/**`
+   - Rust source outside the `crates/editor-shell/**` files listed above
+   - Cargo manifests or lockfiles
+   - workflows, dispatch automation scripts, schemas, architecture-lint
+     rules/config, scheduler config, or unrelated generated artifacts
+
+   **Current-state claims / falsification to include in the TASK packet:**
+   - Claim: the current public route-menu command handler is
+     `EditorShell::route_menu_command`.
+     Falsifying searches:
+     `git grep -n "pub fn route_menu_command" -- crates/editor-* editor/`
+     -> exactly one definition in `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`;
+     `git grep -n -E "impl EditorShell|impl EditorState|route_menu_command" -- crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`
+     -> the definition is inside `impl EditorShell`, with no `impl
+     EditorState` match in that file.
+   - Claim: extension commands are currently captured but not executed.
+     Falsifying search:
+     `git grep -n -E "drain_extension_menu_commands|extension_menu_commands|future plugin/action executor|extension menu command captured" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`
+     -> current FIFO field, one-shot drain, capture log, and retention test
+     are present.
+   - Claim: editor-shell currently has no plugin runtime/discovery/loading path
+     to wire safely in this task.
+     Falsifying search:
+     `git grep -n -E "PluginHost|PluginContext|runtime-wasmtime|plugin-discovery|rge_kernel_plugin_host|rge-runtime" -- crates/editor-shell editor/rge-editor`
+     -> no matches; exit 1 is the expected no-match result.
+   - Claim: the previous failed route-menu owner defect must not recur.
+     Falsifying search before and after edits: run the stale-symbol grep named
+     in the ISSUE-347 TASK packet against `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`,
+     `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `change.md`; no
+     matches are expected, and exit 1 is the expected no-match result.
+
+   **Required behavior:**
+   - Add a small editor-shell-owned extension-command executor seam for
+     commands already captured by `EditorShell::route_menu_command`.
+   - The seam must accept only extension commands (`Command::Custom` and
+     `Command::Plugin`) and must never receive core commands.
+   - Keep the existing capture boundary clear: core commands stay routed by
+     `EditorShell::route_menu_command`; extension commands are captured first,
+     then drained to the executor seam in FIFO order when a handler is
+     configured.
+   - Missing-handler behavior must be explicit and non-fatal. It may preserve
+     the existing observable FIFO/drain behavior, but it must not silently drop
+     extension commands.
+   - Handler failure or unhandled results must be non-fatal, must not run
+     document handlers, and must continue processing later extension commands
+     unless the task discovers an existing local pattern that strongly dictates
+     otherwise.
+   - Tests must use an injected/synthetic handler. Do not introduce a real
+     plugin host, plugin discovery, plugin loading, WASM runtime, capability
+     manifest, async executor, or sandbox integration.
+
+   **Done criteria:**
+   - A configured synthetic handler receives `Command::Plugin` and
+     `Command::Custom` activations in FIFO order after menu routing.
+   - Core commands are not delivered to the extension executor seam.
+   - No-handler behavior is covered and keeps extension activations observable
+     rather than silently dropping them.
+   - Failure/unhandled behavior is covered and does not prevent later extension
+     commands from being processed.
+   - Existing document/menu handlers still behave unchanged for representative
+     core commands such as Save and Toggle Command Palette.
+   - The docs/task bookkeeping records that this task adds only the
+     editor-shell execution seam and does not wire real plugin runtime,
+     discovery, or loading.
+
+   **Verification required:**
+   - Focused editor-shell tests covering extension-command executor FIFO,
+     no-handler behavior, unhandled/failure behavior, and core-command
+     non-delivery.
+   - `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check`
+   - `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib`
+   - `cargo check -p rge-editor-shell --lib`
+   - `git diff --check`
+
+   **Halt conditions:**
+   - The implementation requires editing plugin runtime/discovery/loading,
+     `kernel/plugin-host`, `runtime/**`, `plugins/**`, or Cargo metadata.
+   - The implementation requires changing `crates/editor-ui` command variants,
+     `crates/editor-egui-host` menu projection/registration behavior, host to
+     shell FIFO semantics, generalized registry execution, keybinding editor
+     behavior, conflict fatality policy, OS clipboard, typed clipboard, CAD
+     graph/projection mutation, or undo/dirty integration.
+   - The executor cannot state the current route-menu owner as
+     `EditorShell::route_menu_command` with confidence.
+   - The final docs/task brief names the stale editor-state route-menu owner
+     instead of `EditorShell::route_menu_command`.

Evidence: `EditorShell::route_menu_command` is the current route-menu owner, extension activations already reach an editor-shell FIFO and one-shot drain, and `crates/editor-shell` / `editor/rge-editor` have no current `PluginHost` / `PluginContext` / runtime/discovery/loading path.  > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-08 (ISSUE-345 command-palette recent ordering): task 100 is implemented. `editor-egui-host` now records successful command-palette activations by `Command::diagnostic_id()` into a host-local, in-memory, most-recent-first id list capped at 16. Blank or whitespace-only filters promote currently projected, enabled recent commands first, then append all remaining projected rows in original order; stale ids are ignored, disabled recent rows are not promoted, and task-98 non-blank fuzzy ranking is unchanged. Blank or whitespace-only palette filters promote currently projected, enabled recent commands first, then append all remaining projected rows in original order; stale ids are ignored and disabled recent rows stay only in the remainder. Non-blank task-98 fuzzy search/scoring is unchanged. No `editor-shell`, `editor-ui`, plugin runtime/discovery, persistence, second command model, Cargo, workflow, scheduler, architecture-lint, or dispatch automation behavior changed.

+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Re-run

+- Claim: the current source's public route-menu command definition is the
+  editor-shell handler `EditorShell::route_menu_command`, not an editor-state
+  handler.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n "pub fn route_menu_command" -- crates/editor-* editor/`

+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Run a source-read-only Phase 9/editor-usability selection audit and produce the
+next safe autonomous implementation follow-up. The executor must compare the
+current docs and current source for the editor/plugin usability backlog, then
+append exactly one bounded task 102 to `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, or record
+`NEEDS_HUMAN` if current evidence does not support a safe bounded follow-up.
+This is a source-read-only planning/selection dispatch: it may update only the
+allowed planning/status/task-list files and generated ISSUE-347 artifacts, and
+it must not implement the selected follow-up.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `plans/BASELINE.md`
+- `Status.md`
+- `HANDOFF.md`
+- `change.md`
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `**/*.rs`
+- `crates/**`
+- `editor/**`
+- `tests/**`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `**/Cargo.toml`
+- `.github/workflows/**`
+- `scripts/**`
+- `tools/**`
+- `schemas/**`
+- `plugins/**`
+- `runtime/**`
+- `*.ps1`
+- `**/*.schema.json`
+- Existing `ai_handoffs/**` files other than this dispatch's generated EXEC
+  packet and sidecar.
+- Existing `ai_dispatch_logs/**` files other than this dispatch's generated
+  queue log.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-347_EXEC_*.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-347_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+- `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-347/**`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_ISSUE-347_*.md`
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New Rust source or tests under `crates/**`, `editor/**`, or `tests/**`.
+- New Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflow files, scripts, schemas, plugin
+  runtime files, architecture-lint files, or generated artifacts outside the
+  ISSUE-347 patterns listed above.
+- Any new handoff packet type other than the generated ISSUE-347 EXEC packet.
+
+### MAY read/search
+
+The executor may read and search current source and docs needed to falsify the
+selection claims, including `crates/editor-*`, `editor/`, `crates/plugin-*`,
+`plugins/`, `crates/cad-*`, `crates/kernel-*`, and adjacent source paths needed
+to prove the chosen candidate is bounded. Reads and searches are allowed; edits
+to those source paths are forbidden.
+
+### Advisory machine-readable scope envelope
+
+<!-- handoff:envelope v1 -->
+MAY_EDIT:
+  - plans/BASELINE.md
+  - Status.md
+  - HANDOFF.md
+  - change.md

+INCIDENTAL_OK: false
+<!-- /handoff:envelope -->
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- A recorded audit of current Phase 9/editor-usability candidates, based on
+  source/doc searches run during this dispatch.
+- Either exactly one new task 102 appended to `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, or a
+  clear `NEEDS_HUMAN` record explaining why no safe bounded implementation
+  follow-up can be selected.
+- If task 102 is appended, it must include explicit `MAY edit`, `MUST NOT
+  edit`, done criteria, halt conditions, and verification gates.
+- If task 102 mentions menu routing or `route_menu_command`, it must refer to
+  `EditorShell::route_menu_command` or an unambiguous editor-shell
+  `route_menu_command` reference. It must not say
+  `EditorState::route_menu_command`.
+- An EXECUTION_REPORT that lists the source/doc search commands run, their
+  important results, the selected candidate, and the verification outputs.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The audit compares all of these candidate classes using current evidence:
+  plugin execution policy beyond command capture; FIFO/menu-click routing and
+  generalized registry execution; conflicts/keybindings/fatal gating;
+  persistent history/favorites; unsaved quit; clipboard; CAD
+  delete/duplicate/undo; and camera UI.
+- The selected follow-up is one bounded implementation task, not a batch or
+  roadmap slice. If a bounded task cannot be defended, the executor records
+  `NEEDS_HUMAN` instead of appending an unsafe task.
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` contains exactly one task numbered 102 after a
+  successful selection path. The task has precise file scope, forbidden scope,
+  done criteria, halt conditions, and verification.
+- `EditorState::route_menu_command` has zero matches in `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+  after the edit. Any route-menu reference in task 102 is corrected to
+  `EditorShell::route_menu_command` or an unambiguous editor-shell
+  `route_menu_command` reference.
+- No Rust source, tests, Cargo files, workflows, scripts, schemas, plugin
+  runtime/discovery/loading code, or existing non-ISSUE-347 artifacts are
+  modified.
+- No Rust build or test is expected. The only required verification is the
+  read/search evidence plus diff/status hygiene gates below.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Required
+
+Every negative or unchanged assertion the executor relies on must include the
+inverse search/read command and observed result in the EXECUTION_REPORT.
+
+- Claim: The current source's public route-menu command definition is the
+  editor-shell handler `EditorShell::route_menu_command`, not an

+    `route_menu_command` definition at line 415, and no `impl EditorState`
+    match in that file.
+  - `git grep -n "route_menu_command" -- crates/editor-* editor/ .ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+    -> current matches are editor-shell docs/callers/tests plus editor UI/host
+    references, with the definition still in
+    `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`; this broad search has no
+    `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` match before task 102 is appended.
+  - `git grep -n "EditorState::route_menu_command" -- crates editor .ai/dispatch.tasks.md plans/BASELINE.md Status.md HANDOFF.md change.md`
+    -> no matches before this TASK revision; exit 1 is the expected no-match
+    result.
+- Claim: This worktree has no current task 102 before execution.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n "^102\\." -- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md` -> no matches before this TASK was authored.
+- Claim: The previous failed attempt's source-symbol defect is not allowed to
+  recur.
+  Falsifying search before and after edits:
+  `git grep -n "EditorState::route_menu_command" -- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md plans/BASELINE.md Status.md HANDOFF.md change.md`
+  -> no matches before this TASK revision, and expected no matches after the
+  executor edits task 102; exit 1 is the expected no-match result.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement the selected task. This dispatch only audits and writes the
+  next task brief.
+- Do not edit Rust, tests, Cargo metadata, workflows, scripts, schemas, plugin
+  runtime/discovery/loading code, or any source file.
+- Do not modify existing handoff/log artifacts from other dispatches.
+- Do not append more than one implementation task. The next task must be
+  exactly task 102, or no task if `NEEDS_HUMAN` is recorded.
+- Do not rely on stale roadmap prose when current source contradicts it. Source
+  evidence wins.
+- Prefer `git grep` or PowerShell `Select-String` if `rg` is unavailable in
+  the execution shell; record the actual commands used.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- Source/doc searches supporting each candidate comparison and the selected
+  task -> expected commands and important results recorded.
+- `git grep -n "pub fn route_menu_command" -- crates/editor-* editor/` ->
+  expected exactly one current definition in
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`.
+- `git grep -n "route_menu_command" -- crates/editor-* editor/ .ai/dispatch.tasks.md`

+  reference reviewed for the correct `EditorShell`/editor-shell ownership.
+- `git grep -n "EditorState::route_menu_command" -- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md plans/BASELINE.md Status.md HANDOFF.md change.md` -> expected no matches, with the non-zero no-match exit recorded as success.
+- `git diff --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `git diff --name-only` -> expected changed tracked files limited to
+  `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, and
+  `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, plus generated ISSUE-347 artifacts.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` -> final output recorded.
+
+No `cargo build`, `cargo test`, formatter, workflow, script, schema, or plugin
+runtime verification is expected because this dispatch must not edit those
+surfaces.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without implementing anything and record `NEEDS_HUMAN`
+if any of the following occur:
+
+- Selecting task 102 would require editing source/tests/Cargo/workflows/scripts
+  /schemas/plugin runtime during this dispatch.
+- Current source evidence contradicts all bounded candidate premises or makes

+- More than one candidate appears equally urgent and cannot be ordered from
+  current evidence without human product direction.
+- Any required edit falls outside the MAY edit surface, or any generated file

+- Verification finds `EditorState::route_menu_command` in the final docs/task
+  brief and the correct current-source reference cannot be stated confidently.
+- A command needed for the audit requires network access, elevated privileges,
+  or destructive cleanup.
+
+## Planner Notes

+Prior control feedback found the previous draft otherwise clean, but task 102
+used the wrong symbol: `EditorState::route_menu_command`. Current source
+evidence shows the handler is `EditorShell::route_menu_command` in
+`crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`. Treat that correction as a hard gate

+The original task 101 brief in `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` names plugin/extension

@@ -1814,3 +1814,5 @@ ew / partial / is_full_revolution / existing geometry tests. **Critical design c
 2026-06-08 (ISSUE-345 command-palette recent ordering). Implemented task 100 in `editor-egui-host`. `EguiHost` now owns a host-local, in-memory most-recent-first list of successful command-palette activation ids from `Command::diagnostic_id()`, capped at 16 and de-duplicated by moving existing ids to the front. Palette activations record at the existing `command_palette_window` return -> `MenuCommandHandoff` enqueue point; main-menu activations do not update recents. Blank or whitespace-only filters promote currently projected, enabled recent commands first, then append all remaining projected rows in original order. Stale ids are ignored, disabled recent rows stay only in the remainder, and non-blank task-98 fuzzy score ordering is unchanged. +### 2026-06-08 - Phase 9 editor-usability task-102 selection audit
+
+**Source-read selection result (ISSUE-347).** Current source supports one
+bounded next implementation task: add an editor-shell extension-command
+executor seam for already-captured `Command::Custom` / `Command::Plugin`
+menu activations. This is intentionally smaller than real plugin runtime,
+discovery, loading, sandboxing, or generalized registry execution.
+
+**Current evidence.**
+- `git grep -n "pub fn route_menu_command" -- crates/editor-* editor/`
+  returns exactly one definition:
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:415`. The surrounding source is
+  inside `impl EditorShell`; the correct ownership is
+  `EditorShell::route_menu_command`.
+- `git grep -n -E "drain_extension_menu_commands|extension_menu_commands|future plugin/action executor|extension menu command captured" -- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`
+  shows the current FIFO, the one-shot drain, and the test that proves
+  extension commands are retained for a future executor.
+- `git grep -n -E "PluginHost|PluginContext|runtime-wasmtime|plugin-discovery|rge_kernel_plugin_host|rge-runtime" -- crates/editor-shell editor/rge-editor`
+  exits 1 with no matches. The editor surface has no current plugin runtime,
+  discovery, or loading path to wire in one bounded step.
+
+**Candidate comparison.**
+- Plugin/extension command execution policy beyond capture is the smallest
+  open gap: the menu/host/shell path already carries extension `Command`
+  values to the shell and currently stops at an inert FIFO.
+- Host-shell FIFO/menu-click replacement and generalized registry execution
+  would cut across the menu architecture and command routing model; defer.
+- Conflict resolution/keybinding editor/fatal gating has diagnostics and
+  conflict projection, but a product policy/editor UI is still broader than
+  one small implementation task.
+- Persistent command-palette history/favorites is explicitly open after task
+  100, but persistence/favorites need storage and UX choices; the in-memory
+  recent-ordering slice already shipped.
+- Unsaved quit has dirty-state observation and quit/close handlers, but
+  prompting and graceful shutdown semantics require UI/dialog policy.
+- OS clipboard / typed clipboard remains broader than the shell-local
+  legacy-blob clipboard; typed/CAD identity cloning is explicitly out of the
+  current clipboard path.
+- CAD delete/duplicate/undo integration remains authoritative-content work:
+  current Delete/Duplicate/Cut/Copy/Paste are wrapper-world legacy-blob
+  operations, not CAD graph/projection/render identity mutations through the
+  command bus.
+- Broader camera UI is beyond the current reset/frame/zoom commands; orbit,
+  pan, and richer camera controls need interaction design.
+
+**Selected follow-up.** Task 102 is the editor-shell extension-command
+executor seam. It should define the shell-owned execution policy and test
+FIFO, unhandled, and failure behavior with an injected handler. It must not
+wire real plugin runtime/discovery/loading, add Cargo dependencies, replace
+the host-to-shell menu FIFO, or rename the route-menu owner away from
+`EditorShell::route_menu_command`.
+
